content.full_text:
June 26, 1991

Charles A. Szychowski  
4215 Mobley Avenue  
La Sierra, CA 92505-1749

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. I-91-283

Dear Mr. Szychowski:

You have requested assistance under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Because your questions are general in nature, we consider your letter to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).2/

You have asked the Commission to send you a list of candidates for the office of U.S. President. The Political Reform Act only regulates California state and local elections. Candidates for federal office (such as the United States President) are regulated by the Federal Elections Commission, 999 E Street Northwest, Washington, D.C. 20463.

You have also asked whether or not you may form an "uncontrolled" committee for a candidate who has not yet filed a Candidate Intention Form 501. With regard to California state and local elections, it is not necessary for an individual to declare his or her candidacy for you to form a committee. I have enclosed a Campaign Information Manual which outlines the procedures for forming a non-controlled committee to support or oppose state or local candidates, as well as the appropriate disclosure forms.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

Charles Szychowski
Page Two

Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any additional questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
